issues.title: `gogo plan ready` reports "all N target(s) already spawned - nothing to do" (exit 0) when spawned==0 was actually caused by genuine launch FAILURES, not idempotency
issues.description: Reproduced live with the real /tmp/gogo-spp binary (isolated GOGO_DATA_HOME, no claude on PATH so every launch attempt fails deterministically via launch.Launch's HasClaude() check). cli/plan.go planReady (~line 390-443) tracks `spawned` and `alreadySpawned`/`invalid` but has no counter for a target whose planLauncher call returned err != nil (the per-target stderr line 'spawn into %s failed: %v' IS printed at line 419, then `continue`s). After the loop, `if spawned == 0 { if len(invalid) > 0 {...} ; print 'plan %s: all %d target(s) already spawned - nothing to do'; return 0 }` — when every failure was a genuine launch error (not an invalid target, not an already-spawned member), alreadySpawned==0 and invalid==0 too, so the code falls into the 'already spawned' branch and prints a FALSE success line with exit code 0, directly contradicting the 'spawn into X failed' stderr lines printed moments earlier. A mixed case (1 already-spawned + 1 real failure) is also mislabeled the same way. REV-005 discipline still holds (no member is ever recorded for the failed target, confirmed: the plan file was byte-for-byte unchanged after the failed run), so this is not a data-corruption bug - but a script/CI consumer checking `$?` after `gogo plan ready` (the documented headless mirror of the interactive accept+spawn) would see exit 0 and a plausible-looking 'nothing to do' line and wrongly conclude the plan was fully accepted, when in fact ZERO work items exist. This is the exact class of misreport REV-003 (review round 1) fixed for the invalid-target case, but the fix did not add a parallel branch for the launch-failure case. Note: the TUI mirror (plans_tab.go finishPlanSpawn) does NOT have this bug - it separately tracks launched/failed and reports 'spawned %d work item(s) (%d failed)', so this is CLI-only (plan.go).
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. In planReady, add a `failed` counter alongside `spawned`/`alreadySpawned`/`invalid`, incremented in the `if err != nil { ...; failed++; continue }` branch. In the `if spawned == 0` block, check `failed > 0` (or `failed+len(invalid) > 0`) before falling through to the 'already spawned' message: when there were genuine failures, print a distinct summary (e.g. 'plan %s: 0 spawned - %d already spawned, %d failed to launch (see errors above)') and return a non-zero exit code, mirroring the invalid-target branch's shape. Add a test with a launcher stub that returns an error for every target (spawned=0, alreadySpawned=0, invalid=0) and assert (a) exit code is non-zero, (b) stdout/stderr never claims 'already spawned', (c) the plan file is unchanged.
issues.fix_summary: cli/plan.go planReady: added a `failed []string` counter, appended in the `if err != nil` launch-failure branch (line ~420). The end-of-loop report now builds a single `problems` summary from `invalid` (unresolved targets, REV-003) AND `failed` (genuine launch failures, TEST-001); when spawned==0 the misleading 'all N target(s) already spawned - nothing to do' success line (and exit 0) is printed ONLY in the pure-idempotent case (no problems). Any launch failure or unresolved target now prints 'gogo plan ready: plan X: nothing to spawn - N already spawned, M failed to launch: <names>' on stderr and returns exit 1. The partial-spawn branch (spawned>0 but some failed/unresolved) likewise names both classes and returns 1. TUI mirror finishPlanSpawn left untouched. Added TestCmdPlanReadyLaunchFailuresReported (plan_test.go): stubbed launcher errors on every un-spawned target -> asserts non-zero exit, stderr names web+api + 'failed to launch', no 'nothing to do' line, and plan stays a draft with zero members (REV-005). Existing idempotent/invalid-target/targetless tests stay green.
issues.repro: Isolated GOGO_DATA_HOME + PATH=/usr/bin:/bin (no claude anywhere) + a project 'app2' with 2 real sources; hand-wrote plan-fail0001.md with targets: srcC, srcD; ran `/tmp/gogo-spp plan ready plan-fail0001 --project app2`. Output: two 'spawn into srcC/srcD failed: claude CLI not found on PATH ...' stderr lines, followed by stdout 'plan plan-fail0001: all 2 target(s) already spawned - nothing to do' and exit code 0. plan-fail0001.md confirmed unchanged (status: draft, no members) after the run.
